records.ety: [From the initial (Hebrew) letters of Torah, Nevi'im, and Kethubim.]

defs: A term used among Jews for the Hebrew Bible; the Old Testament.
quotes: The Hebrew Bible is divided into three parts: (1) The Torah, “Law,” or Pentateuch. (2) The Prophets . . . (3) The Kethubim, or the “Writings,” generally termed Hagiographa.
notes: Although Christians use the term “Old Testament”, this term implies the superseding force of the “New Testament”, not recognized as revelation by the Jewish faith.
